seo.title: Building Data Extraction with Vercel AI SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build data extraction with Vercel AI SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building data extraction pipelines with Vercel AI SDK enables seamless structured output from unstructured documents using schema-guided LLM calls in streaming UIs. Guardrails are essential to mitigate partial hallucinations in streams, inconsistent schemas across providers, and unvalidated tool results that can corrupt downstream data pipelines. Vex integration ensures schema compliance and field accuracy for reliable production deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: Data Extraction Architecture with Vercel AI SDK
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture starts with document ingestion into a Vercel Edge Function, where Vercel AI SDK's `generateText` or `streamText` invokes an LLM with a structured output schema. The LLM extracts fields like invoice amounts or entity names into Zod-validated objects, streamed to React Server Components for real-time UI updates. Post-processing validates completeness before persisting to a database like Vercel Postgres.

Key components include the AI SDK's `createStructuredOutput` for schema enforcement, provider adapters (OpenAI, Anthropic), and streaming UI hooks. Failures occur when partial streams hallucinate or schemas drift across models, breaking type consistency.

Vex middleware wraps `generateText`/`streamText` to verify outputs against the schema before rendering, catching issues at the edge without blocking streams.

risks.name: Streaming responses with partial hallucinations
risks.description: LLM generates plausible but incorrect field values mid-stream, corrupting UI before completion
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic verification on stream chunks against document context

risks.name: Tool call results not validated before display
risks.description: Unvalidated extraction results render in UI despite schema violations

risks.name: Inconsistent structured output across providers
risks.description: Different LLMs produce varying field completeness and types for same schema
risks.mitigation: Vex cross-provider normalization and completeness scoring

risks.name: Edge function timeouts on complex agent tasks
risks.description: Long document processing exceeds Vercel Edge limits, dropping incomplete extractions
risks.mitigation: Vex async verification with timeout-aware partial result handling
